items.headline: Deni Avdija Shines with 30-Point Double-Double in Playoff Debut!
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija delivered a sensational performance in his NBA playoff debut, recording a 30-point, 10-rebound, 5-assist double-double, along with a block and a steal, though his Portland Trail Blazers fell to the San Antonio Spurs 111-98 in Game 1 of their series. Avdija has been on a scoring tear, hitting at least 25 points in eight consecutive games.

items.headline: Jaden Ivey Waived by Bulls After Controversial Comments, Reveals Depression Battle
items.summary: Chicago Bulls guard Jaden Ivey has been waived by the team following controversial social media statements regarding Pride Month and the LGBTQ+ community. Post-release, Ivey courageously opened up about his struggle with depression, which prompted widespread support from the NBA fanbase.

items.headline: Star Injury Watch: Durant Questionable, Doncic & Reaves Out for Crucial Game 2 Matchups
items.summary: The Houston Rockets may be without star forward Kevin Durant (right knee contusion), listed as questionable for tonight's Game 2 against the Los Angeles Lakers. The Lakers, meanwhile, will be missing Luka Dončić (left hamstring strain) and Austin Reaves (left oblique muscle strain). Other key players sidelined include Fred VanVleet and Steven Adams for the Rockets, Joel Embiid for the 76ers, and Damian Lillard for the Blazers.

items.headline: Magic Stun Pistons in Game 1 Upset!
items.summary: The Orlando Magic secured a surprising Game 1 victory over the top-seeded Detroit Pistons, leading the series 1-0.

items.headline: Thunder Dominate Suns in Playoff Opener
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder overwhelmed the Phoenix Suns in Game 1, taking a 1-0 series lead with a commanding win.

items.headline: Celtics Rout 76ers to Take Game 1
items.summary: The Boston Celtics delivered a dominant performance against the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 1, securing a large victory.

items.headline: Hawks Rally to Stun Knicks, Tie Series at 1-1
items.summary: CJ McCollum led a late surge as the Atlanta Hawks came back to defeat the New York Knicks, evening their series.

items.headline: Cavs Take 2-0 Lead Over Raptors
items.summary: Donovan Mitchell and James Harden powered the Cleveland Cavaliers to a 115-105 win over the Toronto Raptors, extending their series lead to 2-0.

items.headline: Timberwolves Overcome Deficit to Even Series with Nuggets
items.summary: Anthony Edwards and Julius Randle led the Minnesota Timberwolves to a comeback victory against the Denver Nuggets, tying their series at 1-1.

items.headline: Spurs Take Game 1 Against Blazers
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs defeated the Portland Trail Blazers in Game 1 of their first-round series.

items.headline: 76ers at Celtics - Game 2
items.summary: The Philadelphia 76ers face the Boston Celtics in Game 2 of their playoff series tonight.

items.headline: Trail Blazers at Spurs - Game 2
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers look to bounce back against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 2 tonight.

items.headline: Rockets at Lakers - Game 2
items.summary: The Houston Rockets and Los Angeles Lakers clash in a pivotal Game 2 of their playoff series.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell (CLE)
items.statContext: Led Cavs to 2-0 series lead vs. TOR.

items.headline: CJ McCollum (ATL)
items.statContext: Fueled Hawks' comeback win vs. NYK.

items.headline: Anthony Edwards (MIN)
items.statContext: Led Wolves past DEN to even series.

items.headline: Jayson Tatum (BOS)
items.statContext: Key to Celtics' blowout win vs. PHI.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (OKC)
items.statContext: Paced Thunder in dominant win vs. PHX.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama (SAS)
items.statContext: Sparked Spurs' Game 1 win vs. POR.

items.headline: Draymond Green Speculates on Steve Kerr's Warriors Future
items.summary: Following the Golden State Warriors' exit from the play-in tournament, Draymond Green has publicly speculated that head coach Steve Kerr may not return next season, stating it 'felt like that was it.'

items.headline: Playoff Picture: Early Series Leads Set the Tone
items.summary: The NBA Playoffs are in full swing, with several teams establishing early control. The Magic, Thunder, and Celtics have all taken 1-0 leads, while the Cavaliers lead their series 2-0. The Hawks and Timberwolves have battled back to tie their respective series at 1-1, promising intense competition ahead.

items.headline: 2026 Trade Deadline Impact: Star Moves Reshaped Contenders
items.summary: Although the trade deadline has passed (February 5, 2026), its effects are evident in the playoffs. Key moves included James Harden to the Cleveland Cavaliers, Anthony Davis to the Washington Wizards, and Jaren Jackson Jr. to the Utah Jazz, significantly altering team dynamics for the postseason.
